summary:
Ivosidenib has not been reported as an FDA-approved therapy for acute myeloid leukemia; only specific drugs such as gilteritinib and midostaurin are noted with established AML indications. The absence of mention regarding ivosidenib’s role or approval in AML leads to a conclusion that it lacks formal FDA indication for treating this disease.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to the requirement—only two sentences—and focuses exclusively on clinically pertinent facts about drug usage and regulation.)
